title: Panicum pubifolium

intro: Panicum pubifolium, also known as Wild Millet or Panic Grass, is a type of annual grass native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Panicum pubifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Wild Millet include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis and sore throats.
